mission.title: kured: improve security of reboot mechanism
mission.description: improve security of reboot mechanism. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify kured troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kured deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kured -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured -n kured --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kured configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kured configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kured -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured
kubectl get configmap -n kured -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kured
```
Hello,

We would really like to use kured but there is reluctance about a fully privileged daemonset that has access to execute arbitrary commands as root on every node.  I have reviewed some options that could allow rebooting more securely.

steps.title: Apply the fix for improve security of reboot mechanism
steps.description:
Current state: I've also implemented the first two todos locally and tested it in my home-cluster. The signal in general reboots the server (Ubuntu 20.04 LTS) gracefully, that looks good. However, with this securityContext the command errors with "permission denied":
```yaml
    securityContext:
  
```yaml
{{- if .Values.configuration.rebootSignal}}
  privileged: false
  allowedCapabilities: ['KILL']
{{- else }}
  privileged: true
  allowedCapabilities: ['*']
{{ end }}
```

steps.title: Confirm improve security of reboot mechanism is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kured -n kured --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kured --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Current state: I've also implemented the first two todos locally and tested it in my home-cluster. The signal in general reboots the server (Ubuntu 20.04 LTS) gracefully, that looks good.
codeSnippets:
Jul 28 22:33:46 el8-test.novalocal systemd[1]: Received SIGRTMIN+5 from PID 1764 (n/a).
...
Jul 28 22:33:47 el8-test.novalocal systemd[1]: Reached target Shutdown.

securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        add:
        - CAP_KILL
        drop:
        - '*'
      privileged: false

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kured installed or the issue environment reproducible.
